name: Speedy Cash
description_short: Speedy Cash Van Nuys, California — Speedy Cash in Memphis offers in-store payday loans up to $425, lines of credit up to $4,000, and title loans up to $...
description_long:
Speedy Cash is a national chain of storefront alternative lending locations. The Memphis Summer Avenue branch (38122) serves the east Memphis corridor, operating Monday through Saturday with extended hours on Mondays and Fridays. The store provides a fully staffed in-person lending experience alongside ancillary financial services, positioning itself as a one-stop shop for consumers outside the traditional banking system.

The store offers three distinct loan products: Payday Loans up to $425 (repaid on or around next payday), a revolving Line of Credit up to $4,000 (draw as needed, repay over time with credit restoring as you pay down), and Title Loans up to $4,000 secured by the borrower's vehicle title. Beyond lending, the location provides check cashing, money orders, wire transfers, and Green Dot Visa Debit Card issuance — a full suite of alternative financial services.

Speedy Cash differentiates itself through its in-store application model. Staff walk applicants through product options, assist with the full application, review loan terms before signing, and answer funding and repayment questions on the spot. Online applications are also available for borrowers who prefer to start remotely. The location's multi-product menu — from a $200 payday advance to a $4,000 title loan — means customers with different cash needs can be served under one roof.

Honest assessment: Speedy Cash serves a real market — consumers with urgent cash needs and limited banking options — but comes with the significant costs typical of alternative lenders. Payday loans carry high effective APRs and are due in full on the next payday, creating a rollover risk for cash-strapped borrowers. Title loans require a clear vehicle title as collateral, meaning default can result in repossession. The Line of Credit is the most flexible product but also requires careful management of an ongoing debt facility. Borrowers should review Tennessee Rates and Terms on the website before committing.
pros: Payday loans available up to $425 with same-day in-store funding
Revolving Line of Credit up to $4,000 lets borrowers draw and repay flexibly
Title Loans up to $4,000 for borrowers needing larger amounts against vehicle equity
In-store staff guide applicants through loan options, paperwork, and repayment terms
Check cashing, money orders, wire transfers, and Green Dot Debit Card available in same visit
Extended weekday hours (until 7pm Mon/Fri) accommodate working-hour customers
Online application option available for those who prefer to start remotely
cons: Payday loans capped at $425 — insufficient for emergencies exceeding that amount
Title loans require a clear vehicle title as collateral; default risks repossession
Closed Sundays — no in-store access on the one day many people have free time for errands
High-cost lending products with APRs typical of payday and title loan industry — rates not disclosed on the store page
Content on the store page references 'Check Into Cash' branding, suggesting templated or cross-brand copy that may not reflect this specific location accurately
best_for: Memphis-area consumers who need $100–$425 fast and will repay on their next payday
Borrowers seeking flexible revolving credit up to $4,000 for fluctuating or ongoing expenses
Vehicle owners who need larger short-term cash and are comfortable using their car title as collateral
Customers who prefer applying in person with staff assistance rather than online
diagnosis: Speedy Cash Summer Avenue is best suited for Memphis consumers who need fast access to small-to-mid-size cash — particularly those who prefer in-person guidance and have a vehicle title or upcoming paycheck to secure the loan. The main caveat is cost: payday and title loans are among the most expensive short-term credit products available, and borrowers who cannot repay on the original due date risk a costly cycle of fees or, for title loans, losing their vehicle.
services: In-store loan document review and signing
